questions.content: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.
options: 27. Richard believes that the main problem with using fossil fuels is A they affect everybody through global warming; B their supply will eventually run out; C they create gases that pollute the air and damage all living things

28. Shirley wants to research nuclear energy because A the fuels used in nuclear energy release radiation even after they have been used; B she wants to know how nuclear waste is disposed of; C of public concerns over nuclear power stations situated close to where they live

29. Regarding other energy sources Richard A is going to research renewable energy sources; B is not going to research renewable energy sources; C has not decided if he is going to research renewable energy sources
30. For the next meeting Shirley will find some information about A the benefits of coal and oil; B alternative energy sources; C nuclear power stations

sections.passage_or_context:
Although we cannot do anything about the plants and animals that are already (31) {{BLANK}} we can do something about the many species in the world today that are in danger of becoming extinct in the future.

The main reason for the growing number of (32) {{BLANK}} species is the destruction of their (33) {{BLANK}} The rapidly (34) {{BLANK}} human population needs more land and resources for (35) {{BLANK}} This is changing and destroying the habitats that plant and animal species need for (36) {{BLANK}}

One way we can save endangered species and permanently (37) {{BLANK}} these habitats is to create and support national parks and reserves. National parks encourage people to enjoy the (38) {{BLANK}} of species without harming them.

People who visit these parks must be sure to obey few rules. These include following fire regulations, leaving (39) {{BLANK}} placing all rubbish in a bin, not picking the flowers and not taking birds’ eggs.

Public (40) {{BLANK}} will help ensure the survival of our plant and animal species.
